clusters.neutral_headline: King’s US visit to go ahead as planned, Trump says – as it happened
bullet_summary: Officials have said that the shooter likely was targeting Donald Trump and other senior administration officials
The word “staged” exploded on social media following the attack, as both right- and left-wing influencers and anonymous accounts spread unfounded conspiracy theories
Pointing to Trump's calm demeanor as well as his presser following the event, FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert said the US president is turning assassination attempts into "political rocket fuel"

items.title: Trump tells 60 Minutes in testy interview he ‘wasn’t worried’ during Washington shooting
items.standfirst: US president calls media ‘horrible people’ after CBS correspondent Norah O’Donnell put to him segments of the suspected gunman’s alleged manifestoDonald Trump spoke with CBS correspondent Norah O’Donnell in an interview that aired Sunday night on 60 Minutes describing his ordeal at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out.A gunman opened fire at the Washington Hilton hotel Saturday night, though he did not breach the basement-level ballroom where Trump was sitting at the time. The president described the events in an even tone, saying that he did not feel particularly alarmed as they unfolded. Continue reading...
items.content: US president calls media ‘horrible people’ after CBS correspondent Norah O’Donnell put to him segments of the suspected gunman’s alleged manifestoDonald Trump spoke with CBS correspondent Norah O’Donnell in an interview that aired Sunday night on 60 Minutes describing his ordeal at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner when shots rang out.A gunman opened fire at the Washington Hilton hotel Saturday night, though he did not breach the basement-level ballroom where Trump was sitting at the time. The president described the events in an even tone, saying that he did not feel particularly alarmed as they unfolded. Continue reading...

items.title: Anti-Trump sentiment being examined as motive for White House press dinner shooting
items.standfirst: Manifesto reportedly written by the suspect had Trump administration officials at top of listTrump tells 60 Minutes he ‘wasn’t worried’ during shooting How the White House press dinner shooting unfolded Investigators are looking into anti-Trump sentiment as being a motive for the attacker who sought to breach the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington DC where the US president and top members of his administration were present.Officials have said that the shooter likely was targeting Donald Trump and other senior administration officials. “We do believe, based upon just a very preliminary start to understanding what happened, that he was targeting members of the administration,” acting US attorney general Todd Blanche said in a TV interview. Continue reading...
items.content: Manifesto reportedly written by the suspect had Trump administration officials at top of listTrump tells 60 Minutes he ‘wasn’t worried’ during shooting How the White House press dinner shooting unfolded Investigators are looking into anti-Trump sentiment as being a motive for the attacker who sought to breach the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington DC where the US president and top members of his administration were present.Officials have said that the shooter likely was targeting Donald Trump and other senior administration officials. “We do believe, based upon just a very preliminary start to understanding what happened, that he was targeting members of the administration,” acting US attorney general Todd Blanche said in a TV interview. Continue reading...

items.title: King’s US visit to go ahead as planned, Trump says – as it happened
items.standfirst: This blog is now closed. See our latest full report on the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinnerDonald Trump took to Trump Social on Sunday to repeat his statement from the night before in which he said the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner was why a White House ballroom was necessary.“What happened last night is exactly the reason that our great Military, Secret Service, Law Enforcement and, for different reasons, every President for the last 150 years, have been DEMANDING that a large, safe, and secure Ballroom be built ON THE GROUNDS OF THE WHITE HOUSE,” Trump wrote.It does appear the suspect was targeting members of the administration … We don’t have specifics yet about particular members of the administration, except that we do understand that that was his goal and his target. Continue reading...
items.content: This blog is now closed. See our latest full report on the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinnerDonald Trump took to Trump Social on Sunday to repeat his statement from the night before in which he said the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner was why a White House ballroom was necessary.“What happened last night is exactly the reason that our great Military, Secret Service, Law Enforcement and, for different reasons, every President for the last 150 years, have been DEMANDING that a large, safe, and secure Ballroom be built ON THE GROUNDS OF THE WHITE HOUSE,” Trump wrote.It does appear the suspect was targeting members of the administration … We don’t have specifics yet about particular members of the administration, except that we do understand that that was his goal and his target. Continue reading...

items.title: A tutor with a manifesto: What we know about the US press gala shooting suspect
items.standfirst: The suspect in the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner shooting, widely named as Cole Tomas Allen, is a tutor with a degree in mechanical engineering who was opposed to President Donald Trump's policies, according to US media reports. In a manifesto sent to his family, the 31-year-old California man described himself as a "Friendly Federal Assassin" and apologised to his family and other "non-targeted people".
items.content: The suspect in the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner shooting, widely named as Cole Tomas Allen, is a tutor with a degree in mechanical engineering who was opposed to President Donald Trump's policies, according to US media reports. In a manifesto sent to his family, the 31-year-old California man described himself as a "Friendly Federal Assassin" and apologised to his family and other "non-targeted people".

items.title: The week in pictures: Trump evacuated at media dinner, Leo XIV in Africa and robots race in Beijing
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump was evacuated after shots were fired at the annual White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington DC, Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed in an Israeli strike, the pope visited inmates at an infamous Equatorial Guinea prison and robots outran humans in a Beijing half marathon. Here is a look back at some of the week’s most striking images.
items.content: US President Donald Trump was evacuated after shots were fired at the annual White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington DC, Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed in an Israeli strike, the pope visited inmates at an infamous Equatorial Guinea prison and robots outran humans in a Beijing half marathon. Here is a look back at some of the week’s most striking images.

items.title: Trump turns assassination attempts into 'political rocket fuel'
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump was evacuated by the Secret Service after a gunman opened fire at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, DC. Pointing to Trump's calm demeanor as well as his presser following the event, FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert said the US president is turning assassination attempts into "political rocket fuel".
items.content: President Donald Trump was evacuated by the Secret Service after a gunman opened fire at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, DC. Pointing to Trump's calm demeanor as well as his presser following the event, FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert said the US president is turning assassination attempts into "political rocket fuel".

items.title: Iran’s top diplomat leaves Pakistan for Russia, Trump says Tehran can talk by phone
items.standfirst: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Sunday squeezed in a trip to Oman before returning to Pakistan and then heading to Moscow for talks in a frantic diplomatic shuffle as mediators pressed to reignite talks between Tehran and the US. But President Donald Trump stuck with his decision to scrap a planned trip by this envoys to Pakistan, saying Iran and the US could talk by phone instead.
items.content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Sunday squeezed in a trip to Oman before returning to Pakistan and then heading to Moscow for talks in a frantic diplomatic shuffle as mediators pressed to reignite talks between Tehran and the US. But President Donald Trump stuck with his decision to scrap a planned trip by this envoys to Pakistan, saying Iran and the US could talk by phone instead.

clusters.title: Syria begins first trial for ousted Assad, allies

bullet_summary: A Syrian court held its first hearing on Sunday in the trial of ousted president Bashar al-Assad and senior officials from his former government, state media said
Reported by France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Syria begins first trial for ousted Assad, allies
items.standfirst: A Syrian court held its first hearing on Sunday in the trial of ousted president Bashar al-Assad and senior officials from his former government, state media said. Assad and his brother Maher al-Assad, who fled Syria, are being tried in absentia, while former security official Atif Najib appeared in court in handcuffs.
items.content: A Syrian court held its first hearing on Sunday in the trial of ousted president Bashar al-Assad and senior officials from his former government, state media said. Assad and his brother Maher al-Assad, who fled Syria, are being tried in absentia, while former security official Atif Najib appeared in court in handcuffs.

items.title: First trial of Assad-era Syrian official starts in Damascus
items.standfirst: Syria begun its first public trial of former Assad-era officials, with ex-security chief Atef Najib appearing in court.
items.content: Syria begun its first public trial of former Assad-era officials, with ex-security chief Atef Najib appearing in court.

clusters.title: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
clusters.neutral_headline: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
bullet_summary: Mali’s Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks targeting military sites, officials said, marking a major blow to the ruling junta
Reported by BBC World, France24

items.title: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
items.standfirst: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.
items.content: A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

items.title: Malian Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed by rebels
items.standfirst: Mali’s Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks targeting military sites, officials said, marking a major blow to the ruling junta. The killing comes amid a surge in violence by jihadist and rebel groups, raising fresh concerns over security in the West African nation. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo reports
items.content: Mali’s Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks targeting military sites, officials said, marking a major blow to the ruling junta. The killing comes amid a surge in violence by jihadist and rebel groups, raising fresh concerns over security in the West African nation. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo reports

items.title: 🔴 Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed in al Qaeda‑linked attack near Bamako, RFI reports

bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, BBC World

items.title: Hungary's Viktor Orbán steps down from parliament after a landslide defeat, vows to rebuild
items.standfirst: Outgoing Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán will not take his seat in parliament following a landslide election loss this month.
items.content: Outgoing Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán will not take his seat in parliament following a landslide election loss this month.

items.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
items.standfirst: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.
items.content: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

items.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
items.standfirst: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.
items.content: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

items.title: Trump cancels his envoys’ Pakistan trip for Iran ceasefire negotiations
items.standfirst: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were to travel to Islamabad to attempt to revive ceasefire negotiationsDonald Trump said he has told US envoys not to go to Pakistan for more talks with Iran, shortly after Tehran’s top diplomat left Islamabad late on Saturday.Trump added to Fox News: “They can call us anytime they want.” The White House on Friday said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would travel to Pakistan’s capital to attempt to revive ceasefire negotiations. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
clusters.neutral_headline: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
bullet_summary: It said the attacks were carried out jointly with the Azawad Liberation Front, a Tuareg-led separatist group
BAMAKO, Mali — Gunmen attacked several locations in Mali's capital and other cities early Saturday in a possible coordinated assault, residents and authorities said

items.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
items.standfirst: Al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claims responsibility for strikes on airport in capital, Bamako and four other citiesIslamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali’s capital and other cities on Saturday in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years.The al-Qaida-linked militant group JNIM claimed responsibility for the attacks on Bamako’s international airport and four other cities in central and northern Mali on its website, Az-Zallaqa. It said the attacks were carried out jointly with the Azawad Liberation Front, a Tuareg-led separatist group. Continue reading...
items.content: Al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claims responsibility for strikes on airport in capital, Bamako and four other citiesIslamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali’s capital and other cities on Saturday in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years.The al-Qaida-linked militant group JNIM claimed responsibility for the attacks on Bamako’s international airport and four other cities in central and northern Mali on its website, Az-Zallaqa. It said the attacks were carried out jointly with the Azawad Liberation Front, a Tuareg-led separatist group. Continue reading...
